vulnerability.title: Verify Permissions on Backup gshadow File
vulnerability.description: To properly set the permissions of /etc/gshadow-, run the command: $ sudo chmod 0000 /etc/gshadow-

triage_decision.reason: Adjusting permissions on a backup gshadow file is a straightforward file permission change that does not affect active authentication files or services. The recommended chmod command follows standard STIG guidance and poses no risk of lockout.
triage_decision.estimated_impact: authentication/authorization affected; Incorrect permissions could expose password hashes; If permissions are set too permissively, unauthorized users may read shadow data
